model_narrative: We plan production, shipping, imports, exports, and capacity expansion for a national steel industry over a sequence of multi-year periods. In each period and at each plant we choose how intensively to run each production process, how much raw material to buy domestically or import, how much mining product to extract at each mine and ship to plants, how much intermediate and final product to ship between plants and out to markets, how much to export, and how much to invest in expanding each productive unit. The objective is to minimize the total discounted cost over the whole horizon, where cost combines annualized investment, raw-material and mining outlays, transport, and imports, net of export revenue.

constraints.name: a[c,
constraints.intent: At every steel plant in every period, each commodity must be supplied in at least the amount it is used up. For a given commodity, plant, and period, add together everything that makes the commodity available there: the net output of the plant's production processes, any of that commodity bought domestically when it is a domestically purchased raw material, any shipped in from mines when it is a mining product, any brought in by import when it is an importable raw material, and any shipped in from other plants when it is an intermediate product. That total must be at least everything that consumes the commodity at the plant: shipments out to other plants when it is an intermediate product, shipments out to markets when it is a final product, and exports when it is an export product.

constraints.name: w[cm,
constraints.intent: At every mine in every period, the extraction of each mining product must cover what is shipped out of that mine. For a given mining product, mine, and period, the total extracted across all cost levels must be at least the total shipped from that mine to all plants.

constraints.name: b[m,
constraints.intent: At every plant in every period, the use of each productive unit must stay within the capacity available to it. For a given productive unit, plant, and period, the capacity consumed by the plant's production processes must not exceed the unit's installed capacity. When the unit is one that can be expanded, the available capacity also includes any expansion capacity that has been added in this period or any earlier expansion period.

constraints.name: w[cm,
constraints.intent: Extraction of a mining product at a given cost level and mine is limited over the whole horizon. For each mining product, cost level, and mine, the total extracted across all periods must not exceed that location's allowed extraction limit.

constraints.name: h[me,
constraints.intent: The capacity added to an expansion unit at a plant in an expansion period is determined by how much investment activity is carried out on each segment of the investment function. For each expansion unit, plant, and expansion period, the capacity added equals the sum over the investment segments of the segment's capacity-per-activity times the investment activity on that segment.

constraints.name: y[me,
constraints.intent: Whether an expansion unit is built at a plant in an expansion period is tied to the total investment activity carried out there. For each expansion unit, plant, and expansion period, the build indicator equals the sum of the investment activity across all segments of the investment function.

constraints.name: x[cf,
constraints.intent: Demand for each final product at each market in each period must be met. For a given final product, market, and period, the amount shipped in from all plants plus the amount imported to that market must be at least the demand there.

constraints.name: e[ce,
constraints.intent: Exports are capped in each period. For each period, the total exports of all export products summed over all plants must not exceed that period's export limit.

constraints.name: z['pig-iron',
constraints.intent: Combined primary iron output is capped at each plant in each period. For each plant and period, the pig-iron process activity plus the sponge process activity must not exceed the allowed limit.

constraints.name: phi
constraints.intent: The total discounted cost ties together the period cost components. It equals the sum over all periods of that period's discount factor times the number of years per period times the period's combined cost, where the combined cost adds the investment, raw-material, transport, and import components and subtracts the export revenue.

constraints.name: phikap[t]
constraints.intent: The investment cost in each period accounts for all expansion activity that is active in that period. For each period, it equals the capital recovery factor times the sum, over every expansion unit, investment segment, plant, and expansion period that is in effect for this period, of the investment cost coefficient times the investment activity.

constraints.name: phipsi[t]
constraints.intent: The raw-material and mining cost in each period adds up domestic purchasing and extraction. For each period, it equals the cost of raw materials bought domestically at every plant, valued at their domestic prices, plus the cost of mining products extracted at every cost level and mine, valued at their extraction costs.

constraints.name: philam[t]
constraints.intent: The transport cost in each period sums the cost of every shipment that period. For each period, it equals the unit transport cost times the flow, added up over: final-product shipments from plants to markets, imported final products delivered to markets, mining-product shipments from mines to plants, exports leaving plants, intermediate-product shipments between plants, and imported raw materials delivered to plants.

constraints.name: phipi[t]
constraints.intent: The import cost in each period values everything brought in from abroad at its import price. For each period, it equals the import price times the imported quantity, summed over imported final products delivered to markets and imported raw materials delivered to plants.

constraints.name: phieps[t]
constraints.intent: The export revenue in each period values all exports at their export prices. For each period, it equals the export price times the exported quantity, summed over every export product and plant.

query: There's a proposal to hold the total number of new units built across the whole horizon to no more than 18. Plug that in — what does the program cost then?
expected_pyomo: model.inv_cap = Constraint(expr=sum(model.y[me, i, te] for me in model.me for i in model.i for te in model.te) <= 18)

When the unit is one that can be expanded, the available capacity also includes any expansion capacity that has been added in this period or any earlier expansion period.

constraints.name: w[cm,
constraints.intent: Extraction of a mining product at a given cost level and mine is limited over the whole horizon. For each mining product, cost level, and mine, the total extracted across all periods must not exceed that location's allowed extraction limit.

constraints.name: h[me,
constraints.intent: The capacity added to an expansion unit at a plant in an expansion period is determined by how much investment activity is carried out on each segment of the investment function. For each expansion unit, plant, and expansion period, the capacity added equals the sum over the investment segments of the segment's capacity-per-activity times the investment activity on that segment.

constraints.name: y[me,
constraints.intent: Whether an expansion unit is built at a plant in an expansion period is tied to the total investment activity carried out there. For each expansion unit, plant, and expansion period, the build indicator equals the sum of the investment activity across all segments of the investment function.

constraints.name: x[cf,
constraints.intent: Demand for each final product at each market in each period must be met. For a given final product, market, and period, the amount shipped in from all plants plus the amount imported to that market must be at least the demand there.

constraints.name: e[ce,
constraints.intent: Exports are capped in each period. For each period, the total exports of all export products summed over all plants must not exceed that period's export limit.

constraints.name: z['pig-iron',
constraints.intent: Combined primary iron output is capped at each plant in each period. For each plant and period, the pig-iron process activity plus the sponge process activity must not exceed the allowed limit.

constraints.name: phi
constraints.intent: The total discounted cost ties together the period cost components. It equals the sum over all periods of that period's discount factor times the number of years per period times the period's combined cost, where the combined cost adds the investment, raw-material, transport, and import components and subtracts the export revenue.

constraints.name: phikap[t]
constraints.intent: The investment cost in each period accounts for all expansion activity that is active in that period. For each period, it equals the capital recovery factor times the sum, over every expansion unit, investment segment, plant, and expansion period that is in effect for this period, of the investment cost coefficient times the investment activity.

constraints.name: phipsi[t]
constraints.intent: The raw-material and mining cost in each period adds up domestic purchasing and extraction. For each period, it equals the cost of raw materials bought domestically at every plant, valued at their domestic prices, plus the cost of mining products extracted at every cost level and mine, valued at their extraction costs.

constraints.name: philam[t]
constraints.intent: The transport cost in each period sums the cost of every shipment that period. For each period, it equals the unit transport cost times the flow, added up over: final-product shipments from plants to markets, imported final products delivered to markets, mining-product shipments from mines to plants, exports leaving plants, intermediate-product shipments between plants, and imported raw materials delivered to plants.

constraints.name: phipi[t]
constraints.intent: The import cost in each period values everything brought in from abroad at its import price. For each period, it equals the import price times the imported quantity, summed over imported final products delivered to markets and imported raw materials delivered to plants.

constraints.name: phieps[t]
constraints.intent: The export revenue in each period values all exports at their export prices. For each period, it equals the export price times the exported quantity, summed over every export product and plant.

query: We're looking at a site-diversification rule that stops any single plant from becoming a mega-site: no individual plant may receive more than three new units in total across the whole horizon, counting every unit type and every window. Apply that ceiling at each plant and report the total cost.

model_narrative: We plan production, shipping, imports, exports, and capacity expansion for a national steel industry over a sequence of multi-year periods. In each period and at each plant we choose how intensively to run each production process, how much raw material to buy domestically or import, how much mining product to extract at each mine and ship to plants, how much intermediate and final product to ship between plants and out to markets, how much to export, and how much to invest in expanding each productive unit. The objective is to minimize the total discounted cost over the whole horizon, where cost combines annualized investment, raw-material and mining outlays, transport, and imports, net of export revenue.

constraints.name: a[c,
constraints.intent: At every steel plant in every period, each commodity must be supplied in at least the amount it is used up. For a given commodity, plant, and period, add together everything that makes the commodity available there: the net output of the plant's production processes, any of that commodity bought domestically when it is a domestically purchased raw material, any shipped in from mines when it is a mining product, any brought in by import when it is an importable raw material, and any shipped in from other plants when it is an intermediate product. That total must be at least everything that consumes the commodity at the plant: shipments out to other plants when it is an intermediate product, shipments out to markets when it is a final product, and exports when it is an export product.

constraints.name: w[cm,
constraints.intent: At every mine in every period, the extraction of each mining product must cover what is shipped out of that mine. For a given mining product, mine, and period, the total extracted across all cost levels must be at least the total shipped from that mine to all plants.

constraints.name: b[m,
constraints.intent: At every plant in every period, the use of each productive unit must stay within the capacity available to it. For a given productive unit, plant, and period, the capacity consumed by the plant's production processes must not exceed the unit's installed capacity. When the unit is one that can be expanded, the available capacity also includes any expansion capacity that has been added in this period or any earlier expansion period.

constraints.name: w[cm,
constraints.intent: Extraction of a mining product at a given cost level and mine is limited over the whole horizon. For each mining product, cost level, and mine, the total extracted across all periods must not exceed that location's allowed extraction limit.

constraints.name: h[me,
constraints.intent: The capacity added to an expansion unit at a plant in an expansion period is determined by how much investment activity is carried out on each segment of the investment function. For each expansion unit, plant, and expansion period, the capacity added equals the sum over the investment segments of the segment's capacity-per-activity times the investment activity on that segment.

constraints.name: y[me,
constraints.intent: Whether an expansion unit is built at a plant in an expansion period is tied to the total investment activity carried out there. For each expansion unit, plant, and expansion period, the build indicator equals the sum of the investment activity across all segments of the investment function.

constraints.name: x[cf,
constraints.intent: Demand for each final product at each market in each period must be met. For a given final product, market, and period, the amount shipped in from all plants plus the amount imported to that market must be at least the demand there.

constraints.name: e[ce,
constraints.intent: Exports are capped in each period. For each period, the total exports of all export products summed over all plants must not exceed that period's export limit.

constraints.name: z['pig-iron',
constraints.intent: Combined primary iron output is capped at each plant in each period. For each plant and period, the pig-iron process activity plus the sponge process activity must not exceed the allowed limit.

constraints.name: phi
constraints.intent: The total discounted cost ties together the period cost components. It equals the sum over all periods of that period's discount factor times the number of years per period times the period's combined cost, where the combined cost adds the investment, raw-material, transport, and import components and subtracts the export revenue.

constraints.name: phikap[t]
constraints.intent: The investment cost in each period accounts for all expansion activity that is active in that period. For each period, it equals the capital recovery factor times the sum, over every expansion unit, investment segment, plant, and expansion period that is in effect for this period, of the investment cost coefficient times the investment activity.

constraints.name: phipsi[t]
constraints.intent: The raw-material and mining cost in each period adds up domestic purchasing and extraction. For each period, it equals the cost of raw materials bought domestically at every plant, valued at their domestic prices, plus the cost of mining products extracted at every cost level and mine, valued at their extraction costs.

constraints.name: philam[t]
constraints.intent: The transport cost in each period sums the cost of every shipment that period. For each period, it equals the unit transport cost times the flow, added up over: final-product shipments from plants to markets, imported final products delivered to markets, mining-product shipments from mines to plants, exports leaving plants, intermediate-product shipments between plants, and imported raw materials delivered to plants.

constraints.name: phipi[t]
constraints.intent: The import cost in each period values everything brought in from abroad at its import price. For each period, it equals the import price times the imported quantity, summed over imported final products delivered to markets and imported raw materials delivered to plants.

constraints.name: phieps[t]
constraints.intent: The export revenue in each period values all exports at their export prices. For each period, it equals the export price times the exported quantity, summed over every export product and plant.

model_narrative: We plan production, shipping, imports, exports, and capacity expansion for a national steel industry over a sequence of multi-year periods. In each period and at each plant we choose how intensively to run each production process, how much raw material to buy domestically or import, how much mining product to extract at each mine and ship to plants, how much intermediate and final product to ship between plants and out to markets, how much to export, and how much to invest in expanding each productive unit. The objective is to minimize the total discounted cost over the whole horizon, where cost combines annualized investment, raw-material and mining outlays, transport, and imports, net of export revenue.

query: The very first window, 1984-86, already scatters each kind of unit across a long list of sites. That front-loaded sprawl worries me — couldn't we hold any single technology to at most two sites in that opening window? What's the trade-off pushing the optimizer to spread each unit type so widely right out of the gate?
